Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.
            
What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits" 
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered
- Don't include generic development practices
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts. 
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```
I'll analyze this codebase and create a comprehensive CLAUDE.md file for future Claude Code instances. Let me start by exploring the project structure and understanding the architecture.

# ce-order

## build

### using jib
```bash
docker login jfrog-platform.eu.aws.rccad.net
./mvnw clean package com.google.cloud.tools:jib-maven-plugin:dockerBuild \
        -Djib.from.image=jfrog-platform.eu.aws.rccad.net/ce-eu-docker-nprd/java-base-image:5a7089f9 \
        -Djib.to.image=ce-order:latest 
docker logout jfrog-platform.eu.aws.rccad.net
```

## run

```bash
docker run -p 8080:8080 \
  -e SPRING_CONFIG_IMPORT=optional:configserver: \
  -e SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=local \
  --name ce-order:latest
```

## test

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8080/ce-order/actuator/health
```

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
	<modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

	<parent>
		<groupId>com.richemont.lrp</groupId>	
		<artifactId>spring-boot-parent</artifactId>
		<version>1.3.0</version>
		<relativePath/> <!-- lookup parent from repository -->
	</parent>

	<groupId>com.richemont.ce</groupId>
	<artifactId>ce-order</artifactId>
	<version>1.2.0</version>
	<packaging>pom</packaging>

	<name>ce-order</name>
	<description>Order management for commerce tools</description>

	<modules>
		<module>ce-order-openapi</module>
		<module>ce-order-app</module>
	</modules>

	<properties>
		<java.version>21</java.version>
	</properties>

	<build>
		<plugins>
			<plugin>
				<groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
				<artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
				<executions>
					<execution>
						<goals>
							<goal>enforce</goal>
						</goals>
						<configuration>
							<rules>
								<banDuplicatePomDependencyVersions/>
								<requireUpperBoundDeps/>
							</rules>
						</configuration>
					</execution>
				</executions>
			</plugin>
			<plugin>
				<groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
				<artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
				<executions>
					<execution>
						<goals>
							<goal>properties</goal>
						</goals>
					</execution>
				</executions>
			</plugin>
		</plugins>
	</build>
</project>

# Setting up a Development Environment for Robot Framework Tests (Windows & macOS)

This document describes how to set up your development environment to run Robot Framework tests on both Windows and
macOS.

## Prerequisites

* **Python:** Robot Framework requires Python. We recommend using Python 3.7 or later. You can download it
  from [python.org](https://www.python.org/downloads/). Make sure to add Python to your system's PATH during
  installation.

* **pip:**  Ensure you have `pip` (the Python package installer) installed. It's usually included with Python.

## Setting up a Virtual Environment (Recommended)

Using a virtual environment is strongly recommended to isolate your project's dependencies and avoid conflicts.

1. **Create a virtual environment:**

Go into your project test folder for example : ce-order-tests

- Windows:
  python -m venv .venv
  .venv\Scripts\activate
- macOS/Linux:
  python3 -m venv .venv
  source .venv/bin/activate

2. **Install Robot Framework and libraries within the virtual environment:**
   Repeat the installation steps (step 1 and 2) after activating the virtual environment.

## Installation

1. **Install Dependencies:**

   See this documentation to install python and run robotframework on your laptop : https://agile.richemont.com/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?spaceKey=RDPFM&title=00+-+Get+Started

   If not already done, setup your pip config to target jfrog repository :

   ```bash
   pip config --user set global.index https://RICHEMONT_MAIL:JFROG_PASSWORD@jfrog-platform.eu.aws.rccad.net/artifactory/api/pypi/ce-eu-python
   ```

   ```bash
   pip config --user set global.index-url RICHEMONT_MAIL:JFROG_PASSWORD@https://@jfrog-platform.eu.aws.rccad.net/artifactory/api/pypi/ce-eu-python/simple
   ```
   
   Once installed / if already installed, run this command line to install dependencies : 

   ```bash
   pip install -r robotframework/requirements.txt --no-cache-dir
   ```

2. **Install required test libraries:**
   Install the libraries your tests depend on. For example, if you use the SeleniumLibrary for web testing:
   pip install robotframework-seleniumlibrary Refer to your test suite's requirements or documentation for specific
   library dependencies.

## Running Tests

1. **Navigate to your test suite directory:**
   Open your terminal or command prompt and navigate to the directory containing your Robot Framework test files (.robot
   files).
2. Edit test_configuration.yaml with clientId and secret

Commercetools client used for running test should have following scopes :

- manage_payments
- manage_tax_categories
- manage_products
- manage_standalone_prices
- manage_orders


3. **Run tests:**
   robot <test_file_or_directory>
   For example:
   robot my_tests.robot
   robot test_suite

## IDE Integration (Optional)

For a better development experience, consider using an IDE with Robot Framework support. Some popular options include:

- VS Code: Install the "Robot Framework Language Server" extension.
- IntelliJ IDEA: Install the "Robot Framework Support" plugin.

## Robot Framework Language Server 

**Debug**

If you are using IntelliJ, you probably have to do the same setup as PyCharm :
https://docs.robotframework.org/docs/getting_started/ide#pycharm

Follow this guide :
https://docs.robotframework.org/docs/getting_started/ide#run-and-debug-robot-framework-tests


**Troubleshoots**

Modify plugin configuration if the test cannot be run. The issue can be caused by the plugin not able to find your python venv.
To fix that, go to the Extension tab of VS code, then click on the settings wheel -> Settings
Once inside the settings find this exact section : 

```
Robot › Language-server: Python
Path to the python executable used to start the Robot Framework Language Server (the default is searching python on the PATH).
Requires a restart to take effect.
```

Provide the path of your .venv folder followed by "/bin/python", here is an example :
~/workspace/git/commercetools/ce-payment/ce-payment-tests/.venv/bin/python

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

CE-Order is a Spring Boot-based order management service for Commercetools that handles order lifecycle, including creation, cancellation, capture, returns, and integration with Global-E for international commerce. The service processes events from Commercetools via Solace messaging and exposes REST APIs for order operations.

## Development Commands

### Build and Package
```bash
# Standard Maven build
./mvnw clean package

# Build with Docker using Jib (requires JFrog authentication)
docker login jfrog-platform.eu.aws.rccad.net
./mvnw clean package com.google.cloud.tools:jib-maven-plugin:dockerBuild \
    -Djib.from.image=jfrog-platform.eu.aws.rccad.net/ce-eu-docker-nprd/java-base-image:5a7089f9 \
    -Djib.to.image=ce-order:latest
docker logout jfrog-platform.eu.aws.rccad.net
```

### Run Application
```bash
# Run locally with Docker
docker run -p 8080:8080 \
  -e SPRING_CONFIG_IMPORT=optional:configserver: \
  -e SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=local \
  ce-order:latest

# Run with Maven
./mvnw spring-boot:run -pl ce-order-app -Dspring.profiles.active=local
```

### Test Commands
```bash
# Run unit tests
./mvnw test

# Run specific test class
./mvnw test -Dtest=OrderControllerTest

# Health check
curl -s http://localhost:8080/ce-order/actuator/health
```

### Robot Framework Tests
```bash
# Setup test environment (from ce-order-tests directory)
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # On macOS/Linux
pip install -r robotframework/requirements.txt

# Run tests (requires test_configuration.yaml setup)
robot robotframework/tests/
```

## Architecture

### Multi-Module Structure
- **ce-order-app**: Main Spring Boot application containing business logic, controllers, and services
- **ce-order-openapi**: OpenAPI specifications and contract-first API definitions
- **ce-order-tests**: Integration tests including Robot Framework and Postman collections

### Core Components

#### Controllers
- **OrderController**: Handles order creation endpoints (currently minimal implementation)
- **GlobalEController**: Manages Global-E integration for international checkout
- **ReturnController**: Processes return requests and resets

#### Service Layer
Key services organized by domain:
- **Order Management**: OrderCreationService, OrderUpdateService, OrderStateService, OrderCancellationService, OrderCaptureService
- **Customer Integration**: CommerceCustomerService, CustomerInformationService, CustomerConsentService
- **Returns**: ReturnService handles create/reset return operations
- **Anti-fraud**: OrderAntifraudService for fraud detection integration
- **Global-E**: GlobalEService for international commerce operations

#### Commercetools Integration
Dedicated client classes for Commercetools API:
- **CTCartClient**: Cart operations including GraphQL queries
- **CTOrderClient**: Order management operations
- **CTCustomerClient**: Customer data operations
- **CTPaymentClient**: Payment processing
- **CTStateClient**: State machine management

#### Event Processing
The service consumes Commercetools events via Solace messaging:
- Payment authorization events trigger order creation
- Payment state transitions are processed for order updates
- Custom field updates are handled for order modifications

### Configuration
- **Spring Profiles**: Use `local` profile for development
- **Context Path**: `/order/v1`
- **Default Port**: 8080
- **OAuth2**: Resource server configuration for API security
- **Solace**: Message broker integration for event streaming

### Key Dependencies
- Spring Boot 3.x with Spring Cloud 2024.0.1
- Commercetools SDK 17.27.0
- MapStruct for object mapping
- Solace Spring Cloud Stream for messaging
- Logbook for HTTP request/response logging
- OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing

## Important Patterns

### Error Handling
- Custom exception types for concurrent modifications (ResourceConcurrentModificationException)
- Problem Details (RFC 7807) for API error responses
- Defined error codes in LrpErrorCodes

### Security
- OAuth2 resource server with scope-based authorization
- PreAuthorize annotations on controller methods
- Tenant-based access control pattern: `SCOPE_order:{tenant}:*:order:create`

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for individual services and mappers
- Controller tests with MockMvc
- Robot Framework for end-to-end testing
- Test fixtures in JSON format under test/resources

### API Contract
- OpenAPI-first approach with generated interfaces
- API specs in ce-order-openapi module
- Swagger UI available at `/order/v1/index.html`

I've created a comprehensive CLAUDE.md file for your CE-Order project. The file includes:

1. **Development Commands**: Build, run, and test commands including Docker/Jib builds and Robot Framework test setup
2. **Architecture Overview**: Multi-module structure, core components (controllers, services, Commercetools clients), event processing via Solace, and configuration details
3. **Important Patterns**: Error handling, security with OAuth2, testing strategy, and API-first approach

The CLAUDE.md focuses on high-level architecture that requires understanding multiple files, avoiding repetitive details that can be easily discovered through file exploration.

# Understand Project

I'll analyze your entire application to understand its architecture, patterns, and how everything works together.

**Phase 1: Project Discovery**
Using native tools for comprehensive analysis:
- **Glob** to map entire project structure
- **Read** key files (README, docs, configs)
- **Grep** to identify technology patterns
- **Read** entry points and main files

I'll discover:
- Project type and main technologies
- Architecture patterns (MVC, microservices, etc.)
- Directory structure and organization
- Dependencies and external integrations
- Build and deployment setup

**Phase 2: Code Architecture Analysis**
- **Entry points**: Main files, index files, app initializers
- **Core modules**: Business logic organization
- **Data layer**: Database, models, repositories
- **API layer**: Routes, controllers, endpoints
- **Frontend**: Components, views, templates
- **Configuration**: Environment setup, constants
- **Testing**: Test structure and coverage

**Phase 3: Pattern Recognition**
I'll identify established patterns:
- Naming conventions for files and functions
- Code style and formatting rules
- Error handling approaches
- Authentication/authorization flow
- State management strategy
- Communication patterns between modules

**Phase 4: Dependency Mapping**
- Internal dependencies between modules
- External library usage patterns
- Service integrations
- API dependencies
- Database relationships
- Asset and resource management

**Phase 5: Documentation Synthesis**
After analysis, I'll provide:
- **Architecture diagram** (in text/markdown)
- **Key components** and their responsibilities
- **Data flow** through the application
- **Important patterns** to follow
- **Tech stack summary**
- **Development workflow**

**Integration Points:**
I'll identify how components interact:
- API endpoints and their consumers
- Database queries and their callers
- Event systems and listeners
- Shared utilities and helpers
- Cross-cutting concerns (logging, auth)

**Output Format:**
```
PROJECT OVERVIEW
├── Architecture: [Type]
├── Main Technologies: [List]
├── Key Patterns: [List]
└── Entry Point: [File]

COMPONENT MAP
├── Frontend
│   └── [Structure]
├── Backend
│   └── [Structure]
├── Database
│   └── [Schema approach]
└── Tests
    └── [Test strategy]

KEY INSIGHTS
- [Important finding 1]
- [Important finding 2]
- [Unique patterns]
```

When the analysis is large, I'll create a todo list to explore specific areas in detail.

This gives you a complete mental model of how your application works.
I'll analyze your CE-Order application comprehensively to understand its architecture, patterns, and how everything works together.

content.text:
## PROJECT OVERVIEW
```
├── Architecture: Microservice-based Order Management System
├── Main Technologies: Spring Boot 3.x, Java 21, Commercetools SDK, Solace Messaging
├── Key Patterns: Domain-Driven Design, Event-Driven Architecture, OpenAPI-First
└── Entry Point: Application.java (Spring Boot)
```

## COMPONENT MAP
```
├── API Layer (REST Controllers)
│   ├── OrderController - Order creation endpoints
│   ├── GlobalEController - International commerce integration  
│   └── ReturnController - Return/refund management
│
├── Service Layer (Business Logic)
│   ├── Order Services
│   │   ├── OrderCreationService - Cart to order conversion
│   │   ├── OrderStateService - State machine management
│   │   ├── OrderUpdateService - Order modifications
│   │   ├── OrderCancellationService - Cancellation logic
│   │   ├── OrderCaptureService - Payment capture
│   │   └── OrderCustomFieldsService - Custom field management
│   │
│   ├── Customer Services  
│   │   ├── CommerceCustomerService - Customer data orchestration
│   │   ├── CustomerInformationService - Profile management
│   │   └── CustomerConsentService - GDPR consent handling
│   │
│   ├── Integration Services
│   │   ├── GlobalEService - Global-E checkout integration
│   │   ├── ReturnService - Return processing
│   │   ├── OrderAntifraudService - Fraud detection
│   │   └── LineItemSplitService - Order line splitting
│   │
│   └── Event Services
│       ├── PaymentStatusStateTransitionEventService
│       └── OrderCustomFieldUpdateEventService
│
├── Integration Layer (External Systems)
│   ├── Commercetools Clients
│   │   ├── CTCartClient - Cart operations
│   │   ├── CTOrderClient - Order management
│   │   ├── CTCustomerClient - Customer data
│   │   ├── CTPaymentClient - Payment processing
│   │   └── CTStateClient - State transitions
│   │
│   ├── Commerce Customer Client
│   │   └── CommerceCustomerClient - External customer API
│   │
│   └── Stock Management
│       └── StockClient - Inventory operations
│
├── Messaging Layer
│   └── Solace Configuration
│       ├── Payment status updates consumer
│       ├── Order custom field updates consumer
│       └── Order state transitions consumer
│
├── Data Mapping Layer
│   └── MapStruct Mappers
│       ├── CartCustomerMapper
│       ├── CartAddressMapper
│       ├── GlobalEAddressMapper
│       ├── GlobalEOrderMapper
│       └── GlobalEProductMapper
│
└── Testing
    ├── Unit Tests - Service/Mapper/Controller tests
    ├── Robot Framework - E2E automation tests
    └── Postman Collections - API testing
```

## KEY INSIGHTS

### 1. **Event-Driven Order Processing**
- Orders are created reactively from Commercetools payment authorization events
- Solace messaging handles asynchronous event processing
- Three main event streams: payment updates, custom field changes, state transitions

### 2. **Multi-Tenant Architecture**
- Tenant isolation via path parameter `/{tenant}/...`
- OAuth2 scope-based authorization: `SCOPE_order:{tenant}:*:order:create`
- Per-tenant Commercetools project configuration

### 3. **Commercetools-Centric Design**
- All order/cart/customer data persisted in Commercetools
- Custom retry logic for concurrent modifications
- GraphQL used for complex cart queries
- Extensive use of expand parameters for performance

### 4. **Domain Separation**
- Clear separation between order management and customer management
- Dedicated mappers for data transformation between systems
- Service layer handles all business logic, controllers are thin

### 5. **International Commerce Support**
- Global-E integration for cross-border transactions
- Multi-currency and multi-language support
- Address mapping for international formats
- Stock validation for global inventory

### 6. **Robust Error Handling**
- Custom exceptions for concurrent modifications
- Problem Details (RFC 7807) for API responses
- Retry mechanisms for transient failures
- Comprehensive logging with Log4j2

### 7. **Configuration Management**
- Spring Cloud Config for external configuration
- Environment-specific profiles (local, dev, prod)
- Properties-based configuration for integrations
- OAuth2 resource server auto-configuration

### 8. **Testing Strategy**
- Unit tests for individual components
- Robot Framework for behavior-driven testing
- Test fixtures in JSON format
- Separate test configuration YAML

### 9. **Code Generation Patterns**
- OpenAPI-first with generated model classes
- MapStruct for compile-time safe mapping
- Lombok for boilerplate reduction
- Maven multi-module for clean separation

### 10. **Performance Optimizations**
- Batch operations where possible
- GraphQL for efficient data fetching
- Expand parameters to reduce API calls
- Async message processing for scalability

8. **Testing Strategy**: Unit tests, Robot Framework for behavior-driven testing, and Postman collections

9. **Code Generation Patterns**: OpenAPI-first with generated model classes, MapStruct for compile-time safe mapping

10. **Performance Optimizations**: GraphQL for efficient data fetching, expand parameters to reduce API calls, async message processing

```bash
# Standard Maven build
./mvnw clean package

    -Djib.to.image=ce-order:latest
```

### Run Application
```bash
# Run with Docker
docker run -p 8080:8080 \
  -e SPRING_CONFIG_IMPORT=optional:configserver: \
  -e SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=local \
  ce-order:latest

# Run with Maven
./mvnw spring-boot:run -pl ce-order-app -Dspring.profiles.active=local
```

### Testing
```bash
# Run unit tests
./mvnw test

# Run specific test class
./mvnw test -Dtest=OrderControllerTest

# Robot Framework tests
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r robotframework/requirements.txt
robot robotframework/tests/
```
